steps.title: Find c from the vertical asymptote
steps.content:
The graph does not intersect the line $x = 4$, which means the function is undefined there. The function becomes undefined when its denominator equals zero:
$$2(4) + c = 0$$
$$c = -8$$

steps.title: Find a and b from the zeros
steps.content:
Since $f(5) = 0$ and $f(6) = 0$, and the denominator is nonzero at both points, the numerator must equal zero at $x = 5$ and $x = 6$. The quadratic numerator therefore has roots 5 and 6:
$$x^2 + ax + b = (x - 5)(x - 6)$$
Expanding:
$$(x - 5)(x - 6) = x^2 - 11x + 30$$
Therefore, $a = -11$ and $b = 30$.
